question: Comment preserver la competence humaine sur le code critique quand l'agent ecrit la majorite du code ?

principles.text: Preserver activement la situational awareness humaine sur les chemins critiques (auth, payment, data deletion, security, architectural boundaries) via : (1) lecture obligatoire ligne-par-ligne avant merge sur fichiers designes critical-path ; (2) rotation 'hands-on days' ou l'humain ecrit du code manuellement ; (3) explications generees par l'agent a relire avant merge ; (4) un pourcentage minimum de code critical-path ecrit manuellement (le seuil >=30% reste CHOIX d'equipe). Fondement theorique : Bainbridge 1983 'Ironies of Automation' — les operateurs out-of-the-loop perdent la competence necessaire pour rattraper les erreurs. Evidence empirique : Shukla et al. 2025 montre +37.6% critical vulnerabilities apres 5 iterations AI sans review active.

sources.name: Bainbridge 1983 - Ironies of Automation (Automatica 19(6), DOI 10.1016/0005-1098(83)90046-8)
sources.quote: the more advanced a control system is, so the more crucial may be the contribution of the human operator

sources.name: Shukla, Joshi, Syed 2025 - Security Degradation in Iterative AI Code Generation (IEEE-ISTAS, arXiv:2506.11022)
sources.quote: 37.6% increase in critical vulnerabilities after just five iterations

sources.name: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 AI Act Article 14 §4(b)
sources.quote: to remain aware of the possible tendency of automatically relying or over-relying on the output produced by a high-risk AI system (automation bias)

sources.name: NIST AI RMF 1.0 GOVERN-3.2 Training competencies
Qodo State of AI Code Quality 2025 - survey

etd_balance.benefices: Shukla +37.6% vulnerabilites sans awareness -> gate humaine essentielle critical paths
etd_balance.risques: Fatigue cognitive, faux sens securite, friction sur taches simples
